title: Elatostema peltifolium
taxonomy: Elatostema peltifolium is classified under the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. The genus Elatostema, to which Elatostema peltifolium belongs, is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Elatostema peltifolium is further categorized into the subfamily Urticoideae, which comprises plants with stinging hairs and nettle-like characteristics. The species is also part of the order Rosales, which includes a diverse group of flowering plants with distinct morphological features.

intro: Elatostema peltifolium, also known as the silver shield fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Elatostema peltifolium include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to contribute to its medicinal properties.
